articles.title: Which states are the most investor friendly? Gujarat tops Niti Aayog index; Maharashtra & Tamil Nadu rank next
articles.summary: The index evaluates states and UTs across eight key pillars: infrastructure, business climate, resources, government policy, regulatory ease, institutional environment, financial health and environmental resilience.

articles.title: Timeless legend: Sir Garfield 'Garry' Sobers, West Indies cricketing great, dies at 89
articles.summary: The cricket world stood still in 1958 when a 21-year-old Sobers hammered a monumental 365 not out against Pakistan. The historic knock stood as the highest individual Test score for 36 years until it was broken by fellow West Indian Brian Lara in 1994.

articles.title: AI won't kill India's IT industry...: Anand Mahindra dismisses fears over AI replacing IT services
articles.summary: Speaking at Tech Mahindra's 39th Annual General Meeting (AGM), Mahindra addressed what he described as the biggest concern surrounding the rise of AI and argued that the technology would increase, not reduce, the importance of IT services companies.

articles.title: Amitabh Bachchan back with Kaun Banega Crorepati Season 18. When, where & how to watch
articles.summary: The underlying premise of the new season is straightforward: when information is universally accessible, competitive advantage belongs to those who can connect the dots.

articles.title: India a step closer to its first demonstration-scale geothermal power project
articles.summary: ONGC has completed two wells in Ladakh to harness geothermal power as part of the Puga Geothermal Project.

articles.title: SpaceX shares fall! Market cap of Elon Musk-led firm set to see $1 trillion drop from all-time high
articles.summary: After dropping as much as 6.9% in early US trading to $122.12 per share, the stock recovered some of its losses. At that level, the company's market value stood at $1.61 trillion, down from its high of $2.64 trillion seen at the close of June 16.

articles.title: Apple overtakes Nvidia to become world's most valuable company again
articles.summary: Apple Inc. has regained its position as the world's most valuable company. Nvidia's stock experienced a sharp decline, reducing its market capitalization significantly. Investors are now favoring companies with strong long-term growth prospects over AI boom beneficiaries.

articles.title: RIL Q2 FY27 results: Reliance Industries beats estimates with record quarterly EBITDA; revenue rises 25% yoy
articles.summary: RIL results: Revenue from operations rose 25% year-on-year to Rs 3.11 lakh crore in the June quarter, compared with Rs 2.48 lakh crore in the same quarter last year. Quarterly EBITDA increased 10% from a year earlier to Rs 54,067 crore.

articles.title: US stock market today: Fresh selling in AI-related stocks drags indices down; oil prices rise
articles.summary: The S&P 500 declined 1.3%, putting it on course for its first weekly loss in three weeks and only its third losing week in the past 16. As of 9:35 a.m. Eastern time, the Dow Jones Industrial Average had fallen 451 points, or 0.9%, while the Nasdaq Composite was down 2.1%.

articles.title: Central Bank of India targets $400 million in FCNR (B) deposits; sees no major impact on asset quality from West Asia conflict
articles.summary: The state-owned lender is planning several outreach programmes around the upcoming festivals targeting its NRI customer base.

articles.title: Here's how Gujarat, Maharashtra, and Tamil Nadu fared in NITI Aayog's Investment Friendliness Index
articles.summary: NITI Aayog Vice Chairman Ashok Lahiri underlined importance of investment in boosting demand, states should use index as an indicator to assess performance

articles.title: Strait of Hormuz closure: Why crude oil prices haven’t spiralled out of control amid US-Iran conflict
articles.summary: Brent crude prices in the spot market briefly surged to around $144 per barrel before easing as markets adjusted. Yet as ADB notes in its report - despite this unprecedented supply shock, crude oil prices did not reach levels seen during the 1973 Arab oil emba…

articles.title: India's current account deficit likely to widen to 1.5% of GDP in FY27 as higher oil prices weigh: Report
articles.summary: India's current account deficit is projected to widen significantly by fiscal year 2027. Higher crude oil and commodity prices will drive this projected increase in the deficit. Merchandise imports grew faster than exports in June, widening the trade gap.

articles.title: World's first electromagnetic hybrid transmission: What MG ADAPT means for India's EV future
articles.summary: JSW MG Motor's new MG ADAPT is India's first multi-NEV platform supporting EV, HEV, PHEV and REEV powertrains. One EV and one PHEV will launch on it in FY27. Here's everything inside.

articles.title: One laptop for the whole family: The 5 best home laptops to buy in India right now
articles.summary: From MacBook Air M5 to Asus Vivobook 16, here are the 5 best laptops for home users in India in 2026 — covering Apple, Windows and 2-in-1 options for every type of family user.

articles.title: Messi is richer than Beckham — but the full story behind their fortunes is more interesting
articles.summary: Messi's personal net worth hits $1.1 billion in 2026 per Forbes. Beckham's billion is mostly family wealth combined with Victoria. Here's how both football legends built their fortunes.

articles.title: Millionaire secrets revealed: The habits that quietly build fortunes
articles.summary: Discover the habits, money rules and investment mindset millionaires use to quietly build wealth. From smart spending to asset creation, these secrets reveal how fortunes grow.

articles.title: Shah Rukh Khan, Deepika, Priyanka: 10 Bollywood stars who built billion-dollar business empires
articles.summary: From SRK's Red Chillies to Priyanka's Anomaly Haircare and Alia's Ed-a-Mamma, here are 10 Bollywood celebrities who built billion-dollar business empires beyond the silver screen.

articles.title: 11,000 rpm madness: Inside the engines that defied common sense
articles.summary: From Bugatti’s 1,800 hp V16 to Mercedes AMG One’s F1 engine, explore the extreme car engines that pushed engineering limits with insane power, speed and sound.

articles.title: Pelé’s ₹47 crore shirt sold: But Maradona still holds the record
articles.summary: Pelé’s 1958 World Cup final shirt sells for ₹47.21 crore, becoming football’s second-most expensive jersey. Here’s why Maradona’s iconic shirt still holds the record.

articles.title: Reliance Jio’s LEO satellites get IN-SPACe nod, found technically sound: Report
articles.summary: Following this assessment, the government can extend regulatory support to Reliance Jio at the international level for securing orbital slots.

articles.title: LPG subsidy bill races beyond budget, may hit Rs 1 lakh crore this fiscal
articles.summary: LPG subsidy spending may surpass one lakh crore rupees in FY27. This projection significantly exceeds the budget's thirty thousand crore rupee allocation. Higher fuel prices and ongoing war uncertainty are driving this increased subsidy burden.

articles.title: A foreigner called India's Vande Bharat Sleeper a "five-star hotel on rails" — here's why he's right
articles.summary: A foreign traveller called India's Vande Bharat Sleeper a "five-star hotel on rails" — and the video went viral. Here's what makes the train special and why the world is noticing.

articles.title: Inside SRK’s ₹37 crore Delhi Home: The memories hidden beyond Mannat’s luxury
articles.summary: Inside Shah Rukh Khan and Gauri Khan’s ₹37 crore Delhi home, discover family memories, personal keepsakes, luxury interiors and the untold stories beyond Mannat.

articles.title: Mumbai-Ahmedabad bullet train update: Progress so far and Japan's E10 plan
articles.summary: India's first bullet train project between Mumbai and Ahmedabad is progressing well. Initial operations will commence using Indian high-speed trains in 2027. Japan's next-generation E10 Shinkansen trains will be introduced later, after 2030.

articles.title: He was offered ₹90 lakh. He said no. The reason left his recruiter speechless.
articles.summary: A candidate rejected a ₹90 lakh job offer — not because of the salary, but because of who he'd report to. The HR recruiter's LinkedIn post on why people choose leaders, not companies, went viral.

articles.title: Too hot for suits? Japan tells employees to wear shorts to work as heatwave changes office rules
articles.summary: The initiative comes as Japan braces for another scorching summer following its hottest summer on record in 2025.

articles.title: India's first private orbital rocket launches July 18 — here's everything about Vikram-1
articles.summary: Skyroot Aerospace's Vikram-1 — India's first private orbital rocket — launches July 18 from Sriharikota at 11:30 AM with four payloads and microscopic gold sculptures of Indian scientists.

articles.title: Hidden silver beneath an abandoned factory? Why 6 people were arrested for stealing tonnes of soil
articles.summary: A tip about trace metals led to an investigation that has so far resulted in six arrests and seized material pending forensic analysis.

articles.title: Niti Aayog's roadmap to turn India into $691 billion bioeconomy
articles.summary: NITI Aayog proposes six national initiatives for India's biotechnology sector. This roadmap aims for a $691 billion bioeconomy by the year 2035. These missions will strengthen capabilities in healthcare and agriculture.

articles.title: Why India's first hydrogen train is special - explained
articles.summary: With the launch of a hydrogen train, India has undoubtedly taken a big leap in the sphere of greener transportation. India’s first hydrogen-powered train has 10 coaches, with the capacity to carry 2,600 passengers. It will run between Jind and Sonipat in Haryana.

articles.title: Gold price prediction: Is it time to buy gold on dips? Check July 17, 2026 outlook
articles.summary: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices are favouring a buy on dips strategy, says Jateen Trivedi, VP Research Analyst - Commodity and Currency, LKP Securities.

articles.title: Asian markets tumble as AI stock sell-off deepens; Nikkei sinks nearly 6%, HSI plunges 2%
articles.summary: Asian stock markets experienced significant selling pressure on Friday. Japan's Nikkei 225 and Taiwan's benchmark saw sharp declines. Concerns over artificial intelligence stock valuations and Middle East conflict weighed on investors.

articles.title: Stock market rally: Sensex rises over 750 points, Nifty50 crosses 24,250 - top reasons for rise
articles.summary: The sharp rally in Indian equities came even as global markets remained under heavy pressure. Japan's Nikkei tumbled more than 5%, while Taiwan's Weighted Index plunged 6% as the sell-off in semiconductor stocks continued.

articles.title: India's first hydrogen-powered train flagged off by PM Modi; check route, timings, how it works
articles.summary: India’s first hydrogen train is a 10-coach passenger offering. According to Indian Railways, globally the few operational or under testing hydrogen trains have 2-3 passenger coaches, making India’s first hydrogen train the longest of its type in the world.

articles.title: Rupee rises 14 paise in early trade as Dalal Street cheers; oil adds limited gains
articles.summary: The rupee opened stronger against the US dollar on Friday, gaining fourteen paise in early trade. Upbeat equity markets provided support for the domestic currency's early gains. However, sustained foreign fund outflows and firm crude oil prices capped further appreciation.

articles.title: Hydrogen train, Rs 25K cr in projects: PM Modi's agenda in Haryana, Chandigarh & Punjab
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will launch development projects exceeding twenty-four thousand eight hundred crore rupees. These initiatives span highways, railways, healthcare, and education infrastructure across three regions.

articles.title: Where are gold and silver prices heading? Inflation, oil and Fed bets hold the key
Crude heads for biggest weekly gain in months amid intensifying Middle East crisis
articles.summary: Oil prices surged as escalating US-Iran military tensions raised supply disruption concerns. Brent crude traded at $85.34 and WTI crude at $80.08 per barrel. Recent US strikes aimed to degrade Iranian military capabilities, while Iran retaliated against regional allies.

articles.title: India’s First Hydrogen-Powered Train Flagged Off by PM Modi: 10 Facts & Photos You Shouldn’t Miss
articles.summary: ​India’s first hydrogen-powered train has been launched! Marking a major milestone for Indian Railways, the country’s first hydrogen was flagged off by Prime Minister Narendra Modi on July 17.
